expectations.text: Organizes output into the 5 canonical buckets (pain points, trigger moments, objections, transformations, standout language)
expectations.evidence: Both products organized under BUCKET 1-5 labels: PAIN POINTS, TRIGGER MOMENTS, OBJECTIONS, TRANSFORMATIONS, STANDOUT LANGUAGE

expectations.text: References scoring reviews 1-5 for quality before analysis
expectations.evidence: Review scoring section assigns 2-5 score to each of 6 reviews before bucket analysis

expectations.text: Surfaces actual quotes from reviews (not just summary)
expectations.evidence: 13 pulled quotes across both products including 'weekend in the mountains', 'too piney for me', 'settles my nerves'

expectations.text: Output is separated/grouped by product (Hearth vs Evergreen)
expectations.evidence: Two separate product sections labeled 'PRODUCT: Hearth Candle' and 'PRODUCT: Evergreen Diffuser' each with own buckets

expectations.text: Surfaces distinct insights per product (not merged analysis)
expectations.evidence: Hearth insights are pre-call nerves/rituals; Evergreen insights are scent longevity/small-space fit — distinct

expectations.text: Captures specific objections (scent fades, too piney, price)
expectations.evidence: Names 'scent fades faster than I'd like', 'too piney for me', and '$38 feels expensive' objections explicitly
